question: Will any U.S. Supreme Court justice announce their retirement before September 1, 2026?

factors.description: Base rate: SCOTUS retirement announcements almost always occur at end of term (late June). The 2026 term ended late June with zero announcements; the natural window has passed. Off-cycle July-August announcements are historically very rare.
Alito (most speculated) confirmed NOT retiring — actively hiring clerks for 2026-27 term, intends to serve into 2027. NPR story was a retracted error.
Thomas also signaled via multiple sources he does not plan to step down in 2026.
Strategic incentive: Republicans may lose Senate in Nov 2026 midterms, creating pressure for an older conservative to retire before losing confirmation power.
Tail risk: sudden health event forcing unexpected retirement among 9 justices (2 aged 76-78) in an 8-week window.

summary: As of July 8, 2026, no Supreme Court justice has announced retirement. The natural announcement window (end of term, late June) has passed with zero announcements. The two most-speculated justices — Alito and Thomas — have both actively signaled they are staying; Alito is hiring clerks for the 2026-27 term and intends to serve into 2027, and the NPR retirement story was a retracted error. Some upward pressure exists from strategic pre-midterm Senate dynamics and health tail risk across 9 justices (2 aged 76-78), but neither speculated target is acting on it. Status quo (NO) is heavily favored over the ~8-week window.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 6%**

The question asks whether any of nine named Supreme Court justices will announce a retirement before September 1, 2026. My research found:

- **No justice has announced** as of July 8, 2026.
- The **natural announcement window (end of term, late June) has already passed** with zero announcements — SCOTUS retirements are almost always announced at the close of the term.
- The two most-speculated justices, **Alito and Thomas, have both actively signaled they are staying**. Alito is hiring clerks for the 2026-27 term and the NPR "retirement" story was a promptly-retracted error.
- Modest counter-pressure exists (strategic pre-midterm Senate dynamics; health tail risk across two justices aged 76-78), but no one is acting on it.

Status quo strongly favors NO. Final probability: **0.06**.
